mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year, creating a negative real rate environment that incentivizes equity allocation and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into real assets.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 Fed funds at 0.14%, inflation expectations rising
regime_hint: post-pandemic monetary accommodation with emerging inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between real rate proxy and forward returns falls below 0.15 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) exceeds 500k persons, signaling robust labor market recovery that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 payroll levels at 142.288M, recovering from pandemic lows
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises fail to produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day forward returns < 0.05 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1.2 million annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, indicating sustained consumer demand and construction activity that supports corporate earnings across multiple sectors, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 housing starts at 1206k, retail sales at 447.7B
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, signaling supply-side weakness and labor market softening that reduces near-term earnings growth expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns as the market reprices growth downward.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 industrial production at 107.08, unemployment at 5.1%
failure_condition: Negative signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between INDPRO weakness and UNRATE elevation becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter exceeds 1.5% annualized rate, indicating sustained economic expansion that supports equity valuations and earnings growth, producing positive 60-day forward returns as the market prices in continued expansion momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2020 GDP at 16324.3B chained 2017 dollars, post-pandemic recovery underway
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth periods fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.08 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative 60-day forward returns as the market reprices earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 unemployment at 5.1%, elevated from pandemic lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Unemployment deterioration produces negative 60-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.06 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.5% while industrial production (INDPRO) grows month-over-month by more than 0.8%, indicating accommodative monetary policy supporting strong supply-side growth that drives positive equity returns over 5 trading days as the market recognizes synchronized expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 Fed funds at 0.14%, industrial production at 107.08
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with accelerating industrial activity
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in < 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between accommodative rates and strong production growth becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1.5% while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.1 million annualized units, signaling synchronized contraction in consumer and construction demand that reduces near-term corporate earnings, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 retail sales at 447.7B, housing starts at 1206k
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or demand shock
failure_condition: Negative demand signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between retail sales weakness and housing starts decline becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in rolling 12-month windows.
